channel.title: Responder TX — Hill Country Flood Ops
channel.description: Flash flood emergencies, forecast river crests, and active notices for the Texas Hill Country. Situational awareness — not a dispatch system; call 911 for emergencies.

item.title: MAJOR crest forecast — Frio River at Tilden (27.8 ft)
item.description: Observed 7.87 ft (no_flooding); forecast crest 27.8 ft MAJOR at 2026-07-22T06:00:00Z. Source: NOAA NWPS.

item.title: MAJOR crest forecast — Nueces River at Cotulla (22.1 ft)
item.description: Observed 7.71 ft (no_flooding); forecast crest 22.1 ft MAJOR at 2026-07-22T18:00:00Z. Source: NOAA NWPS.

item.title: [CRITICAL] ASHERTON HAS CRESTED: peak 31.56 ft MAJOR (~7:30 PM CT) — 95.6% of the all-time 1935 record — now RECEDING, 31.49 at 8:30 PM CT. Highest water of the modern gauge era. Major flooding persists through the fall; evacuated areas stay evacuated until water drops well below major stage.
item.description: NWPS ASRT2: peak observed 31.56 ft (~00:30Z), 8:30 PM CT reading 31.49 and easing — crest confirmed passed. Event peak stands ~1.4 ft below the Jun 1935 all-time 33 ft; ~6 ft above the 1991-era record. Forecast holds near 31.5 overnight then a slow fall — MAJOR-stage water persists for days on the recession; downstream Nueces reaches continue receiving the wave. Do not re-enter low-lying areas until officials clear them. — Asherton — Nueces River (Dimmit Co.) (Dimmit Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] LOWER FRIO — Derby (DBYT2) HOLDING ~20.5 ft MAJOR and running BELOW its ~21.1 ft forecast crest: observed has been flat at 20.4–20.5 for 2+ hours (20.48 at 11:30 AM CT), so the model looks to be over-forecasting again. Still solid MAJOR flooding — riverside ranches, low crossings, and equipment in the Derby reach stay flooded — but the acute rise has stalled short of the forecast.
item.description: ['NWPS DBYT2 11:30 AM CT: observed 20.48 ft MAJOR (major stage 17), essentially flat since ~9 AM (20.4–20.51 band) versus the ~21.1 ft crest NWPS still forecasts around 1 PM CT — consistent with the other overnight over-forecasts that under-ran. Treat 21.1 as an upper bound, not a lock. Keep equipment and livestock off the Derby river bottom and avoid low crossings; MAJOR-stage water persists on the falling/flat limb even if it never reaches the forecast peak.'] — Lower Frio River — Derby / Tilden reaches (Frio Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Falls City EASING: the Cibolo crest came in well UNDER the revised 23.6 ft — observed only ~19.9 ft MINOR at 10:15 AM CT and forecast to fall to ~19.5 ft. Another over-forecast that under-ran; the record/moderate threat has passed, though low-lying Cibolo bottoms may stay wet.
item.description: NWPS FCTT2 10:15 AM CT: observed 19.92 ft MINOR, forecast 19.5 ft minor 17:00Z (~12 PM CT) — the crest topped out at minor, well below the 23.6 ft moderate (and the earlier 25.2 ft major) forecasts. Livestock-loss reach note stands but the acute threat has passed. — Falls City — Cibolo Creek (FM 791 / CR area) (Karnes Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] PECOS AT LANGTRY — EASING, warnings expired: the Val Verde flash-flood warnings expired at 11:15 AM CDT. Langtry (LTRT2) crested ~16.7 ft MODERATE and is receding (15.83) — it came in well under the 21.7 ft MAJOR the model forecast. Still moderate flooding on the Pecos; low-water crossings in the Langtry/Comstock reach may stay wet. Threat past peak.
item.description: ['NWPS LTRT2 11:15 AM CT: observed 15.83 ft (moderate) and falling — the Pecos wave peaked ~16.66 ft moderate mid-morning, well short of the 21.7 ft MAJOR forecast (another model over-forecast, consistent with overnight). Both NWS Austin/San Antonio flash-flood warnings for Val Verde (Central river reach + East Central heavy rain) EXPIRED at 11:15 AM CDT with no renewal. Same water that crested Pandale (~15.9 ft) overnight. Low-water crossings on the falling limb can still be flooded — do not cross — but the acute flash-flood threat has passed.'] — Pecos River — Langtry / Central Val Verde (Val Verde Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] LLANO RIVER — wave RESOLVING: it crested well below the model's MAJOR forecast and both gauges are receding. NWS has now revised the crests DOWN to match — Llano (LLAT2) 18.8 ft falling, fcst cut to ~17.5 MODERATE; Mason (MLRT2) 18.6 ft falling, fcst cut to ~22.9 MINOR. No major crest coming. Flood Warnings may linger on the falling limb; low crossings can still be wet.
item.description: ['Resolved read 2:57 AM CT: the Llano wave under-ran its forecast and is receding on both gauges, and NWPS has now revised its own forecasts down to confirm it — Llano at Llano (LLAT2) forecast cut from 25 ft MAJOR to ~17.5 ft MODERATE, Llano near Mason (MLRT2) from ~29 MAJOR (then 26 moderate) down to ~22.9 ft MINOR. The much-higher overnight model crests never materialized. NWS Flood Warnings for Mason/Llano counties may still run on the recession; low-water crossings can remain flooded as the river drops. Acute threat has passed.'] — Llano River — Mason → Llano (Mason Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] RECOVERY + DOWNSTREAM WATCH: no active TX flash-flood emergencies, but officials warn of danger the NEXT 24-48 HRS as the Nueces crest walks downstream — the Nueces is the greatest ongoing threat
item.description: Gov. Abbott (Uvalde, 5 PM CT): rain easing but floodwaters moving downstream will keep rivers rising and threatening communities for 24-48 hrs; Nueces is currently the greatest threat. 570+ rescued statewide+local (90+ boats, 20 aircraft, 200 high-profile vehicles). Board is in anticipation/recovery posture: Asherton major crest ~30 ft Sat evening (>1 mi wide forecast, FM 190/Hwy 85 impassable), Derby 16.2 rising to 21 overnight, Tilden Mon. Stay out of receding rivers and off flooded crossings. — Texas Hill Country — regional (Regional Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] BRIDGE DESTROYED: FM 481 (Old Eagle Pass Rd) Nueces River bridge washed out — Uvalde↔Eagle Pass link severed; expect long detours
item.description: TxDPS: a section of the FM 481 bridge over the Nueces southwest of Uvalde was washed away after up to 20 in of rain in Uvalde Co. FM 481 links US 90 (Uvalde) to US 57 (toward Eagle Pass) — closure forces lengthy detours for residents, ranchers, ag and commercial traffic. Do not approach the washout. Use TxDOT DriveTexas for detours. — FM 481 (Old Eagle Pass Rd) at Nueces River, SW of Uvalde (Uvalde Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] Zavala Co EVACUATIONS as the Nueces wave arrives: Curtis Bourne Rd (Crystal City) told to evacuate; Averhoff Landing & Chula Vista subdivisions prepare — Asherton major flooding forecast, FM 190 / Hwy 85 may go impassable
item.description: Zavala County Sheriff: floodwaters expected to reach homes near Curtis Bourne Rd (Crystal City); Averhoff Landing and Chula Vista residents warned to prepare. Downstream Nueces wave; NWS forecasts major flooding near Asherton, river possibly >1 mi wide over FM 190, Hwy 85 impassable. Follow county evacuation orders; never drive flooded roads. — Crystal City — Nueces River downstream corridor (Zavala Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Sonora/Sutton FF EMERGENCY EXPIRED 1:15 PM — area remains under a CONSIDERABLE flash flood warning to 3:30 PM; flooded roads and debris persist, do not resume low-water crossing travel
item.description: NWS: the catastrophic-tagged Sutton Co emergency was allowed to expire at 1:15 PM CT; a CONSIDERABLE-tagged FFW (issued 12:33) covering Sutton/Crockett/Schleicher runs to 3:30 PM. Recovery posture with active runoff — crossings stay dangerous for hours. — Sonora — Sutton County (western Edwards Plateau) (Sutton Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Junction crest PASSED at ~30.3 ft (high moderate — the 31.3 MAJOR forecast did not verify); Llano now forecast to fall to ~26 ft — stay clear of riverbanks during recession
item.description: NWPS JNCT2 12:08 PM CT: observed 30.27 moderate at/just past crest, forecast now a falling limb to ~26 ft. Peak came in ~1 ft under the major threshold. Bank instability and debris hazards persist during recession; downstream Llano reaches still rising. — Junction — Llano River (Kimble Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] Frio River at Concan crested MAJOR (~15.4 ft) — Garner State Park corridor flooding; avoid river crossings, campground/low-water areas dangerous
item.description: NWPS CONT2-area gauge: observed 12.76 moderate and peaking ~15.4 major around 11 AM CT. Westward storm round now flooding the upper Frio. Verify park/campground status before any routing. — Concan / Garner State Park corridor — Frio River (Uvalde Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] FLASH FLOOD EMERGENCY EXTENDED: Ozona/Crockett Co through 2:15 PM CT — flash flooding ongoing; second extension of the westward round
item.description: NWS San Angelo extended the Crockett Co emergency-worded warning to 2:15 PM (prior 11:45). Sutton/Sonora emergency runs to 1:15 PM. Johnson Draw caution stands. — Ozona and surrounding areas — east-central Crockett County (Crockett Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] Sutherland Springs FALLING (33.9 ft) — forecast secondary bump CANCELED (fcst revised to 33.1); homes-cutoff avoided; FM 539-south roadway still submerged until further drop
item.description: NWPS SUPT2 9:40 AM CT: observed 33.9 (moderate) and falling, forecast 33.1 — the ~35.5 morning re-rise did not materialize. Keep the card open for the roadway; downgrade posture to damage-assessment as it clears. — Sutherland Springs / Falls City — Cibolo Creek (Wilson & Karnes Co.) (Wilson Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Rain winding down for all — dry by the weekend; flood threat shifts fully downstream: Crystal City/Asherton/Cotulla (Nueces), Derby/Fowlerton/Choke Canyon (Frio), Spring Branch (Guadalupe), Stockdale/Falls City/Kenedy (Cibolo/San Antonio R.)
item.description: KSAT 8 AM CT: rain winding down everywhere; crests continue moving through lower basins into the weekend. Statewide: 2,000+ responders working, 230+ rescues so far (Texas Tribune live blog). — All flooded river corridors (Guadalupe, Pedernales, Nueces, Frio) (Multi Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Spring Branch crest has PASSED — Guadalupe fell out of flood categories and is receding fast; damage assessment and re-entry checks begin along the Canyon Lake corridor
item.description: Boil water notice remains for Canyon Lake service area. Verify campground/park re-entry only with officials. — Spring Branch / Canyon Lake / Guadalupe River State Park corridor (Comal Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] BOIL WATER NOTICE for Canyon Lake: flood debris/sediment spiked turbidity at the Canyon Lake Shores Treatment Plant intake — bottled/potable water staging may be needed
item.description: Issued to protect public health during elevated turbidity. Coordinate potable water for shelters and vulnerable residents in the service area. — Canyon Lake — water system service area (Comal Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] Uvalde County: rescue phase winding down as Nueces/Frio crests pass — shift to welfare checks and resupply of cut-off pockets
item.description: Nueces below Uvalde falling from ~27.8 ft record-area crest; Frio below Dry Frio falling. TDEM resupply running. Officials still ask residents to stay off roads. — Uvalde County — Nueces/Leona/Frio river areas (Uvalde Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Uvalde access INTERMITTENT: US 90 into the county reopened then re-closed at E Main St; Garner Field Rd one lane each direction — verify drivetexas.org before every trip
item.description: TDEM (Chief Nim Kidd): food and water being delivered to cut-off parts of Uvalde. 85+ boats, 20 aircraft, 200 high-profile vehicles deployed statewide. Shelters: Flores Elementary, SW Texas College, Dalton Elementary, Red Cross Fairplex. Overlay radius is an operator estimate. — Uvalde — city and surrounding areas isolated (Uvalde Co.)

item.title: [CRITICAL] US 90 CLOSED both directions at Sabinal and west of Uvalde — pavement torn up in places; corridor flooding Hondo to Del Rio; US 57, FM 140, FM 3352, FM 1581 also closed
item.description: TxDOT: at least 125 roadways affected and 87 CLOSED across Central Texas due to flooding, damage and debris. Frio/Uvalde/Zavala closures include US 57, FM 140, FM 3352, FM 1581. Route via drivetexas.org; assume low crossings impassable. — US-90 corridor — Sabinal / Uvalde / Hondo to Del Rio (Uvalde Co.)

item.title: [HIGH] Flash flood emergency: large and deadly flood wave moving down the Pedernales; riverside residents must move to high ground
item.description: Flood wave reached Johnson City 3:41 PM Thu; gauge now falling (20.1 ft from 24.3 peak). Blanco County FF emergency still active — many roads around the river remain closed; 30+ rescued near Fredericksburg Thursday morning. — Pedernales River — central Blanco / southern Gillespie (Blanco Co.)
